title: Nvidia tops $40B in AI equity commitments for 2026, anchored by OpenAI stake
subtitle: The chipmaker has now combined commercial supply deals with equity stakes across roughly two dozen AI infrastructure companies this year alone.
author: AESOP AI Engine
body: Nvidia has committed more than $40 billion to equity stakes in AI companies in 2026 — a tally CNBC and TechCrunch surfaced on May 9. Roughly $30 billion of that is the company's bet on OpenAI; the rest is spread across seven multi-billion-dollar deals in public companies, including up to $3.2 billion in Corning for optical components and up to $2.1 billion in data-center operator IREN, plus participation in around two dozen private rounds.

The pattern matters because Nvidia is no longer just selling GPUs to the AI economy — it is taking equity in the companies that buy those GPUs, the companies that build the data centers to house them, and the companies that supply the optics and power to run them. The same dollar shows up twice: once as revenue for Nvidia, once as an investment Nvidia owns.

That structure is unusual for a chip company and concentrates more of the AI buildout inside one balance sheet than any prior compute cycle. It also raises questions about how to value Nvidia's revenue if a meaningful share of demand is from customers it is funding. Regulators have not intervened, but the FTC and DOJ are watching adjacent deals; the EU's AI Office has flagged compute concentration in its quarterly market notes.

For learners: when one company sits at the center of a stack — chips, capital, customers, and supply chain — read the financials carefully. 'Round-trip' deals (vendor funds customer, customer buys from vendor) are common in early platform eras and are not inherently bad, but they make growth numbers harder to interpret. Always ask: where would this revenue come from if the vendor stopped writing checks?

sources.title: Nvidia has already committed $40B to equity AI deals this year — TechCrunch
Nvidia embraces AI investor, topping $40 billion in equity bets — CNBC
Nvidia's AI Investment Bets Top $40 Billion In 2026 — Benzinga
